name: cmlibs-exporter
documentation:
# CMLibs Exporter

CMLibs exporter classes. This software can be found on PyPi and installed with the following command:

```
pip install cmlibs.exporter
```

When using the thumbnail exporter there are additional requirements for hardware or software rendering.
To install the thumbnail exporter with support for hardware rendering install *cmlibs.exporter* with:

```
pip install 'cmlibs.exporter[thumbnail_hardware]'
```

To install the thumbnail exporter with support for software rendering install *cmlibs.exporter* with:

```
pip install 'cmlibs.exporter[thumbnail_software]'
```

To force the use of the software renderer even when hardware rendering is available, set an environment variable like so:

```
OC_EXPORTER_RENDERER=osmesa
```

either in the environment the exporter is run in or before calling the export thumbnail method.

## Distribution

This software uses regex to extract the version number information from the package. The version number for this package is stored in 'src/cmlibs/exporter/\_\_init\_\_.py'

modules.name: cmlibs
documentation: Namespace package initialisation file.

modules.name: cmlibs.exporter.baseimage
documentation: Base class for exporting an Argon document to a JPEG image.

classes.name: cmlibs.exporter.baseimage.BaseImageExporter

methods.name: cmlibs.exporter.baseimage.BaseImageExporter.__init__
documentation:
---
param output_prefix: The prefix to apply to the output.
param output_target: The target directory to export the visualisation to.
---

methods.name: cmlibs.exporter.baseimage.BaseImageExporter.export
documentation:
Export the current document to *output_target*. If no *output_target* is given then
the *output_target* set at initialisation is used.

If there is no current document then one will be loaded from the current filename.

```
param output_target

Output directory location.
```

methods.name: cmlibs.exporter.baseimage.BaseImageExporter.export_image
documentation: Export graphics into an image format.

A base class for exporting visualisation described by an Argon document to JPEG.
By default the export will be use PySide6 to render the scene.
An alternative is to use OSMesa for software rendering.
To use OSMesa as the renderer either set the environment variable
OC_EXPORTER_RENDERER to 'osmesa' or not have PySide6 available in the
calling environment.

The Zinc Scene object to be exported.

methods.name: cmlibs.exporter.base.BaseExporter.set_document
documentation:
Set the document to export.

```
param document

Set the document to export.
```

documentation:
Set filename.

```
param filename

The filename for the Argon document.
```

methods.name: cmlibs.exporter.base.BaseExporter.set_parameters
documentation:
Set the parameters for this exporter.
The parameters must have values for:

- numberOfTimeSteps
- initialTime
- finishTime
- prefix

```
param parameters

A dict of parameters.
```

methods.name: cmlibs.exporter.base.BaseExporter.load
documentation:
Loads the named Argon file and on success sets filename as the current location.
Emits documentChange separately if new document loaded, including if existing document cleared due to load failure.

:return:  True on success, otherwise False.

modules.name: cmlibs.exporter.flatmapsvg
documentation:
Export an Argon document to source document(s) suitable for the generating
flatmaps from.

functions.name: cmlibs.exporter.flatmapsvg._calculate_cervical_thoracic_boundaries
documentation:
From the markers determine the cervical and thoracic boundary.
We flip the final value to match the flipping done in calculating
the 'upright' vagus position.
Returns None if the markers used in the calculation are not found.

```
param markers

A list of markers from which to determine the regions.

return

The cervical thoracic boundary in SVG image units.
```

functions.name: cmlibs.exporter.flatmapsvg._is_annotation_csv_file
documentation:
Check if the given CSV reader represents an annotation CSV file.

Args:
    csv_reader (csv.reader): The CSV reader to check.

Returns:
    bool: True if it represents an annotation CSV file, False otherwise.
